blocks:
Surah Al-Ma'arij

Makkan, its number of verses is forty-four verses بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) سَأَلَ سائِلٌ بِعَذابٍ واقِعٍ ** (1) An asker asked for a punishment bound to befall— Meaning: A caller (داع) called for it (به), in the sense that he sought its immediate occurrence (استدعاه). And it was recited (وقرئ) as سأل (sa'ala) with the alif (الألف), and this is either a dialect (لغة) concerning it, or it is derived from flowing (السّيلان).

(٢) لِلْكافِرينَ. (2) for the faithless. In al-Kafi, narrated definitively (مقطوعاً): Verily, it was revealed concerning those who are faithless (لِلْكافِرينَ) regarding the wilayah (ولاية) of Ali (peace be upon him). He said: "Thus, by Allah, did Gabriel (جبرئيل) descend with it upon Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, and thus, by Allah, is it affirmed in the Mushaf (مصحف) of Fatimah (peace be upon her)."

I say: What supports this is what passed concerning the reason for its revelation in Surat al-Anfal (Chapter 8), at the Almighty's saying: وَإِذْ قالُوا اللهُمَّ إِنْ كانَ هذا هُوَ الْحَقَّ مِنْ عِنْدِكَ فَأَمْطِرْ عَلَيْنا حِجارَةً مِنَ السَّماءِ أَوِ ائْتِنا بِعَذابٍ أَلِيمٍ. (And when they said, 'O Allah, if this is the truth from Your presence, then rain down upon us stones from the sky, or bring us a painful punishment.')

And al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): That He was asked about the meaning of this verse, and He said: "A fire that emerges from the West (المغرب) and an angel drives it from behind until it reaches the dwelling of Banu Sa'd ibn Hammam near their mosque. It will not leave a dwelling belonging to Banu Umayyah except that it burns it and its inhabitants, nor will it leave a dwelling in which there is a remnant (وتر) of the Family of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them) except that it burns it. That is al-Mahdi (المهديّ)." He said in another narration (حديث آخر): When the two cavalry forces lined up on the Day of Badr, Abu Jahl raised his hand and said: "O Allah, [if Muhammad] is the one who severs the ties of kinship (أقطعنا للرّحم) and brings us what we do not know, then let the punishment surprise him." So Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, revealed: سَأَلَ سائِلٌ بِعَذابٍ واقِعٍ لِلْكافِرينَ لَيْسَ لَهُ دافِعٌ (An asker asked for a punishment bound to befall— for the faithless. There is none to avert it) to repel it (يردّه).

(٣) مِنَ اللهِ ذِي الْمَعارِجِ ** (3) From Allah, the Lord of the Ascents. The possessor of the ascents (المصاعد), and they are the degrees (الدرجات) in which the good word (الكلم الطيّب) and the righteous deed (العمل الصالح) ascend, and in which the believers rise in their spiritual journey (سلوكهم), and the angels and the Spirit ascend therein. (٤) تَعْرُجُ الْمَلائِكَةُ وَالرُّوحُ إِلَيْهِ فِي يَوْمٍ كانَ مِقْدارُهُ خَمْسِينَ أَلْفَ سَنَةٍ ** (4) The angels and the Spirit ascend to Him in a day whose measure is fifty thousand years. This is a new clause (استئناف) to explain the loftiness of those ascents (المعارج) and the distance of their reach, by way of likening the spiritual realm (الملكوت) to the physical realm (الملك) regarding the temporal extension (الامتداد الزّماني) from which the spiritual realm (الملكوت) is purified (or transcendent).

In al-Qummi, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, He said: تَعْرُجُ الْمَلائِكَةُ وَالرُّوحُ ** (The angels and the Spirit ascend) to Him, in the morning of the Night of Decree (ليلة القدر), from the presence of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله and the Successor (الوصيّ) عليه السلام.

In al-Ihtijaj, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام: He mentioned the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, saying: He was taken on the Night Journey (أسرى به) from the Sacred Mosque (المسجد الحرام) to the Farthest Mosque (المسجد الأقصى), a distance of a month's journey, and He was ascended (عرج به) through the spiritual realm of the heavens (ملكوت السماوات), a distance of fifty thousand years, in less than a third of a night, until He reached the Leg of the Throne (ساق العرش).

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq عليه السلام: "Indeed, the Resurrection (القيامة) has fifty stations (موقفاً), each station lasting a thousand years." Then He recited the verse فِي يَوْمٍ (in a day).

In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله: He was asked, "O Messenger of Allah, how long is this Day?" He replied: "By Him in Whose Hand is the soul of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله, it will be lightened for the believer, so that it will be lighter for him than a prescribed prayer (صلاة مكتوبة) that he performs in the world." And narrated from Imam al-Sadiq عليه السلام: "If anyone other than Allah were to undertake the Reckoning (الحساب), they would remain in it for fifty thousand years before they finished. But Allah, the Glorified, will finish that in an hour."

And narrated from Him عليه السلام, He said: "That Day will not reach its midpoint until the people of Paradise take their midday rest (يقيل) in Paradise, and the people of the Fire take their midday rest in the Fire."

(٥) فَاصْبِرْ صَبْراً جَمِيلاً ** (5) So bear with a graceful patience. Al-Qummi said: Meaning, due to the denial of those who deny that this will occur. (٦) إِنَّهُمْ يَرَوْنَهُ بَعِيداً ** (6) They see it as far off, From possibility (الإِمكان). (٧) وَنَراهُ قَرِيباً ** (7) but We see it as near. From occurrence (الوقوع). (٨) يَوْمَ تَكُونُ السَّماءُ كَالْمُهْلِ ** (8) The day the sky is like molten copper, Al-Qummi said: He said, the molten lead and copper; likewise, the sky will melt. (٩) وَتَكُونُ الْجِبالُ كَالْعِهْنِ ** (9) and the mountains are like carded wool, Like dyed wool of various colors. It was said: Because the mountains are of different colors, so when they are pulverized and scattered in the air, they resemble carded wool when the wind scatters it. (١٠) وَلا يَسْئَلُ حَمِيمٌ حَمِيماً ** (10) and no friend will inquire about [the welfare of] a friend, And no relative will ask a relative about his condition. And it was read (وقرئ) based on the passive voice (على البناء للمفعول). (١١) يُبَصَّرُونَهُمْ (11) though they shall be brought face to face. Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) that He said: He says they will recognize them, yet they will not question one another on the Day when the guilty one will wish to ransom himself from the punishment of that day with his children, (١٢) وَصاحِبَتِهِ وَأَخِيهِ. (12) his spouse and his brother, (١٣) وَفَصِيلَتِهِ ** (13) and his kinsfolk who shelter him, It was said: And his clan (عشيرته) from whom he separated. الَّتِي تُؤْوِيهِ ** (who shelter him) means: who embrace him in lineage and during hardships. And Al-Qummi said: It is his mother who bore him. (١٤) وَمَنْ فِي الْأَرْضِ جَمِيعاً ثُمَّ يُنْجِيهِ. (14) and everyone on the earth, all together, so that it may deliver him. (١٥) كَلَّا ** (15) No indeed! It is a raging fire, A deterrent (ردع) for the guilty one from wishing (الودادة), and an indication that ransom will not save him. Indeed, the Fire is pure flame. (١٦) نَزَّاعَةً لِلشَّوى ** (16) stripping away the scalp, And it was read (وقرئ) with the nasb (accusative case). And al-shawā (الشّوى) means the extremities (الاطراف), or it is the plural of shawāh (شواة), which is the skin of the head. Al-Qummi said: It strips away his eyes and blackens his face. (١٧) تَدْعُوا مَنْ أَدْبَرَ وَتَوَلَّى ** (17) calling him who turned his back and retreated, He said: It drags him towards itself. (١٨) وَجَمَعَ فَأَوْعى ** (18) and amassed [wealth] and hoarded it. And he gathered wealth and placed it in a container (وعاء) and hoarded it out of greed and hope. Al-Qummi said: He gathered wealth, buried it, and hoarded it (وعاه), and did not spend it in the way of Allah. (١٩) إِنَّ الْإِنْسانَ خُلِقَ هَلُوعاً ** (19) Indeed, man was created covetous: Severely greedy (شديد الحرص), lacking patience (قليل الصّبر). (٢٠) إِذا مَسَّهُ الشَّرُّ جَزُوعاً ** (20) when evil befalls him, he is fretful, Al-Qummi said: He said, al-sharr (الشرّ) (evil) is poverty (الفقر) and destitution (الفاقة). (٢١) وَإِذا مَسَّهُ الْخَيْرُ مَنُوعاً ** (21) and when good befalls him, he is grudging, He said: Wealth (الغنى) and abundance (السّعة). (٢٢) إِلَّا الْمُصَلِّينَ. (22) except the worshippers, Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) that He said: Then He made an exception and described them by their best deeds. (٢٣) الَّذِينَ هُمْ عَلى صَلاتِهِمْ دائِمُونَ ** (23) those who are constant at their prayers, He said: He means that if a person obligates upon himself something from the supererogatory prayers (النوافل), he remains constant upon it. And in al-Khisal, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): Those who make up what they missed of the night during the day, and what they missed of the day during the night.

(٢٤) وَالَّذِينَ فِي أَمْوالِهِمْ حَقٌّ مَعْلُومٌ. (24) and those in whose wealth there is a known right, (٢٥) لِلسَّائِلِ وَالْمَحْرُومِ (25) for the beggar and the deprived,

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sajjad (peace be upon him): The known right (الحقّ المعلوم) is something he takes out of his wealth, which is neither from the Zakat nor from the obligatory Sadaqah (charity). It is something he takes out of his wealth; if he wishes, he gives more, and if he wishes, he gives less, according to the measure of what he owns. With it, he maintains kinship (رحماً), strengthens the weak, carries the burden of the dependent (كلًّا), and maintains connection with a brother of his for the sake of Allah, or for a calamity (نائبة) that befalls him. And there are other narrations with the same meaning.

And narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): الْمَحْرُومِ ** (the deprived) is al-Muhārif (المحارف), who has been deprived of the fruit of his labor (كدّ يده) in buying and selling. And in a narration: الْمَحْرُومِ ** (the deprived) is he whose intellect has no defect (ليس بعقله بأس), but provision (الرّزق) has not been expanded for him, and he is Muhārif (محارف).

(٢٦) وَالَّذِينَ يُصَدِّقُونَ بِيَوْمِ الدِّينِ. (26) and those who affirm the Day of Retribution, In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), He said: [It refers to] the advent (بِخُرُوجِ) of the Qā’im (peace be upon him).

**(٢٧) وَالَّذِينَ هُمْ مِنْ عَذابِ رَبِّهِمْ مُشْفِقُونَ ** (27) and those who are apprehensive of the punishment of their Lord— fearful for their souls.

(٢٨) إِنَّ عَذابَ رَبِّهِمْ غَيْرُ مَأْمُونٍ ** (28) indeed the punishment of their Lord is not a thing to be assured against— This is an interjection (اِعْتِرَاضٌ) indicating that it is not proper for anyone to feel secure from the punishment of Allah, even if he exerts himself greatly in His obedience.

(٢٩) وَالَّذِينَ هُمْ لِفُرُوجِهِمْ حافِظُونَ. (29) and those who guard their private parts,

(٣٠) إِلَّا عَلى أَزْواجِهِمْ أَوْ ما مَلَكَتْ أَيْمانُهُمْ فَإِنَّهُمْ غَيْرُ مَلُومِينَ. (30) except from their spouses or those whom their right hands own, for then they are not blameworthy.

(٣١) فَمَنِ ابْتَغى وَراءَ ذلِكَ فَأُولئِكَ هُمُ العادُونَ ** (31) But whoever seeks [anything] beyond that—it is they who are the transgressors. Its interpretation has already passed in Surat al-Mu’minūn.

(٣٢) وَالَّذِينَ هُمْ لِأَماناتِهِمْ وَعَهْدِهِمْ راعُونَ ** (32) and those who are guardians of their trusts and covenants, preserving [them]. And it was recited (لِأَمَانَتِهِمْ) [in the singular].

(٣٣) وَالَّذِينَ هُمْ بِشَهاداتِهِمْ قائِمُونَ ** (33) and those who stand by their testimonies, they do not conceal [them] nor deny [them]. And it was recited (بِشَهَادَاتِهِمْ) [in the plural] due to the variation in types [of testimonies].

(٣٤) وَالَّذِينَ هُمْ عَلى صَلاتِهِمْ يُحافِظُونَ ** (34) and those who are watchful of their prayers. meaning they observe its conditions (شَرَائِطِهَا) and its manners (آدَابِهَا).

In al-Kafi and al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), He said: This [Ayah] refers to the obligatory prayer (الْفَرِيضَة), and الَّذِينَ هُمْ عَلى صَلاتِهِمْ دائِمُونَ ** (and those who are constant at their prayers) refers to the supererogatory prayer (النَّافِلَة). And narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him): Those are the people of the fifty prayers (الْخَمْسِينَ صَلَاة) among our followers (شِيعَتِنَا).

(35) أُولئِكَ فِي جَنَّاتٍ مُكْرَمُونَ. (35) They will be in gardens, honoured.

(36) فَما لِ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا قِبَلَكَ حولك مُهْطِعِينَ مسرعين. (36) What is the matter with the faithless that they are rushing toward you, (near you (حولك), rushing (مسرعين)).

(37) عَنِ الْيَمِينِ وَعَنِ الشِّمالِ عِزِينَ (37) from the right and the left, in groups? It is said (قيل) that it means scattered groups (فرقاً شتّى), the plural of عِزَة. And al-Qummi says it means sitting (قعود).

And in al-Ihtijaj, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), when he mentioned the hypocrites (المنافقين), he said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم continued to reconcile with them (يتألّفهم), bring them near, and seat them to his right and his left, until Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, permitted Him to distance them by His saying: وَاهْجُرْهُمْ هَجْراً جَمِيلاً (And forsake them with a graceful forsaking) and by His saying: فَما لِ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا قِبَلَكَ مُهْطِعِينَ (What is the matter with the faithless that they are rushing toward you), and the verses that follow (الآيات).

(٣٨) أَيَطْمَعُ كُلُّ امْرِئٍ مِنْهُمْ أَنْ يُدْخَلَ جَنَّةَ نَعِيمٍ ** (38) Does every man among them hope to be admitted into the Garden of bliss? without faith (إيمان). It is said that this is a denial of their saying: "If what He says were true, we would have a better share (حظّاً) in it than them, just as (we have) in the world (الدنيا)."

(٣٩) كَلَّا ** (39) Certainly not! A repudiation of this hope (الطمع). ** إِنَّا خَلَقْناهُمْ مِمَّا يَعْلَمُونَ ** (39) Certainly not! We created them from what they know. Al-Qummi said: From a sperm-drop (نطفة) then a clinging mass (علقة).

I say: This means that the creature created from the filthy sperm-drop (النّطفة القذرة) is not qualified for the realm of sanctity (عالم القدس) unless he perfects himself through faith (الإِيمان) and obedience (الطاعة) and acquires the celestial morals (الأخلاق الملكيّة).

(٤٠) فَلا أُقْسِمُ ** (But nay! I swear) The particle لا (lā) is added for emphasis (لِلتَّأْكِيد), and this is common (شَائِع) in their speech (كَلَامِهِم). Al-Qummiyy said: Meaning, I swear بِرَبِّ الْمَشارِقِ وَالْمَغارِبِ ** (by the Lord of the easts and the wests) He said: He meant the easts of winter and the easts of summer, and the wests of winter and the wests of summer.

And in al-Ma'ani, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah, He said: It has three hundred and sixty easts (مَشَارِق) and three hundred and sixty wests (مَغَارِب). The day on which it rises (تُشْرِقُ) is a day to which it does not return except in the following year (مِنْ قَابِل), and the day on which it sets (تَغْرُبُ) is a day to which it does not return except in the following year (مِنْ قَابِل).

And in al-Ihtijaj, narrated from Him (peace be upon him) regarding it (the Ayah), He said: It has three hundred and sixty constellations (بُرُوج). It rises every day from one constellation (بُرْج) and sets in another, and it does not return to it except in the following year (مِنْ قَابِل) on that day. إِنَّا لَقادِرُونَ. (We are indeed able)

(٤١) عَلى أَنْ نُبَدِّلَ خَيْراً مِنْهُمْ ** (41) that We may replace them with [others] better than them, and We are not to be outdone. meaning, We destroy them and bring forth a creation better (أمثل) than them. وَما نَحْنُ بِمَسْبُوقِينَ [meaning] We are not to be outdone (بمغلوبين) if We intend that. (٤٢) فَذَرْهُمْ يَخُوضُوا وَيَلْعَبُوا حَتَّى يُلاقُوا يَوْمَهُمُ الَّذِي يُوعَدُونَ. (42) So leave them to indulge [in their vain talk] and play until they encounter their day which they are promised. (٤٣) يَوْمَ يَخْرُجُونَ مِنَ الْأَجْداثِ ** (43) The day they emerge from the graves swiftly, as if they were racing toward a target, from the graves (من القبور), swiftly (سِراعاً), hastening (مسرعين). كَأَنَّهُمْ إِلى نُصُبٍ يُوفِضُونَ ** towards something set up (منصوب) for worship or a sign (علم), they hasten (يسرعون). Al-Qummi said: Towards the caller (الدّاعي) they rush (يبادرون). And nuṣub (نُصُب) was recited with two ḍammahs (بضمّتين) [on the nūn and ṣād] as a plural (على الجمع).

(٤٤) خاشِعَةً أَبْصارُهُمْ تَرْهَقُهُمْ ذِلَّةٌ ذلِكَ الْيَوْمُ الَّذِي كانُوا يُوعَدُونَ ** (44) their eyes cast down, abasement will cover them. That is the Day which they were promised. in the world (الدنيا).

In Thawab al-A'mal (ثواب الأعمال), narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Increase the recitation of Sa'ala Sa'il (Surat al-Ma'arij), for whoever increases its recitation, Allah the Almighty will not question him on the Day of Resurrection concerning any sin he committed, and He will house him in Paradise (الجنّة) with Muhammad (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace). And in al-Majma' (المجمع), narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): the like of it.
